name: OWASP HIGH Vulnerability Fix — mercury-services-commons
description: Analysis and fix plan for 11 HIGH OWASP findings in booking module's transitive dependencies from mercury-services-commons. Covers Netty removal (replacing with AWS CRT), Dropwizard upgrade, Logback override, and Jetty EOL handling.

entries.summary: Completed full analysis of 11 HIGH OWASP findings in booking module from mercury-services-commons transitive deps
entries.detail:
11 HIGH CVEs from 3 sources:
- 9 Netty CVEs (4.1.124.Final) from cloud-sdk-aws explicit pins via netty-nio-client
- 1 Jetty CVE (11.0.26, EOL) from Dropwizard 4.0.16 via commons
- 1 Logback CVE (1.5.18) from Dropwizard 4.0.16 via commons

OWASP evidence heuristic differences between individual JAR vs shaded JAR scanning explain why cloud-sdk-aws report didn't show Netty HIGHs but booking did.

entries.summary: Netty removal feasibility: HIGH — only 1 production file, 3 test files impacted. aws-crt-client already a dependency.
entries.detail:
Netty removal impact analysis completed:

PRODUCTION IMPACT (1 file):
- AwsHttpClientWrapper.java: defaultAsyncClient() uses NettyNioAsyncHttpClient → replace with AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient

Only S3 Transfer Manager uses async clients. All other services (SQS, SNS, SSM, SES, DynamoDB) are sync-only with ApacheHttpClient.

TEST IMPACT (3 files):
- TransferManagerFactoryTest.java: Netty builder + isInstanceOf assertions
- S3ClientFactoryTest.java: Partially migrated (has both Netty and CRT references)
- AwsStorageConfigTest.java: createNettyHttpClient() helper + parameterized tests
- AwsHttpClientWrapperTest.java: Already uses CRT — no changes needed

POM IMPACT: Remove netty-nio-client + 9 explicit io.netty:* version pins (10 deps total). aws-crt-client already declared.

RECOMMENDATION: Remove Netty entirely, replace with AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient (already in tests, AWS-recommended for S3).

entries.summary: Fix plan: Remove Netty entirely (replace with CRT), upgrade Dropwizard 4.0.17, override Logback 1.5.32, suppress Jetty EOL CVE
entries.detail:
Fix plan documented at cloud-sdk-aws/docs/2026-04-16-owasp-high-vulnerability-analysis-and-fix-plan.md

Revised execution order (Netty REMOVAL instead of upgrade):
1. Remove netty-nio-client + 9 io.netty:* deps from cloud-sdk-aws/pom.xml
2. Replace NettyNioAsyncHttpClient → AwsCrtAsyncHttpClient in AwsHttpClientWrapper.defaultAsyncClient()
3. Update 3 test files to use CRT instead of Netty
4. Upgrade Dropwizard 4.0.16 → 4.0.17 in root pom.xml
5. Override Logback to 1.5.32 if DW 4.0.17 doesn't fix it
6. Handle Jetty 11.x EOL CVE via suppression or risk acceptance
7. Publish commons SNAPSHOT, rebuild booking, re-run OWASP
